locked_facts.balance_condition: P/Q = R/S where P and Q are wire resistances proportional to lengths
locked_facts.balance_formula: X/R = (100 - l) / l where l is balancing length in cm

ncert_truth_statements: A metre bridge works on the principle of the Wheatstone bridge and uses a one-metre long uniform resistance wire stretched on a wooden board
The balance condition for a metre bridge is X/R = (100 − l)/l, where l is the balancing length in centimetres and R is the known resistance
At balance, the galvanometer shows no deflection and no current flows through it, giving an accurate measurement of the unknown resistance X
The metre bridge is most sensitive when all four arms of the equivalent Wheatstone bridge have nearly equal resistance, placing the balance point near the middle of the wire
End resistance errors arise from the resistance of connecting wires at the two ends of the metre bridge wire and must be accounted for in precise measurements
